description: The `CompressionQueryTransformer` compresses a conversation history and a follow-up query into a standalone query using a large language model. This is particularly useful for long conversation histories where maintaining context is important.
exampleUsage:
To use the `CompressionQueryTransformer`, first build a `ChatClient` and optionally a custom `PromptTemplate`. Then, use the builder pattern to create an instance of the transformer. Finally, call the `transform` method with a `Query` object containing the conversation history and follow-up query to obtain a compressed `Query` object. Example:

```java
Query query = Query.builder()
        .text("And what is its second largest city?")
        .history(new UserMessage("What is the capital of Denmark?"),
                new AssistantMessage("Copenhagen is the capital of Denmark."))
        .build();

QueryTransformer queryTransformer = CompressionQueryTransformer.builder()
        .chatClientBuilder(chatClientBuilder)
        .build();

Query transformedQuery = queryTransformer.transform(query);
```

A technique termed Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) has emerged to address the challenge of incorporating relevant data into prompts for accurate AI model responses. The approach involves a batch processing style programming model, where the job reads unstructured data from your documents, transforms it, and then writes it into a vector database. At a high level, this is an ETL (Extract, Transform and Load) pipeline. The vector database is used in the retrieval part of RAG technique. As part of loading the unstructured data into the vector database, one of the most important transformations is to split the original document into smaller pieces. The procedure of splitting the original document into smaller pieces has two important steps: The next phase in RAG is processing user input. When a user’s question is to be answered by an AI model, the question and all the “similar” document pieces are placed into the prompt that is sent to the AI model. This is the reason to use a vector database. It is very good at finding similar content. A TranslationQueryTransformer uses a large language model to translate a query to a target language that is supported by the embedding model used to generate the document embeddings. If the query is already in the target language, it is returned unchanged. If the language of the query is unknown, it is also returned unchanged. This transformer is useful when the embedding model is trained on a specific language and the user query is in a different language.
